---
title: "Impacto de la IA en los estudiantes"
output:
  html_document:
    code_folding: hide
    df_print: paged
author:
  - Luis-Felipe Guajardo Torres
  - Pablo Morales Carvallo
  - rancisco Bahamondes Castro
  - Gabriel Lafertte Cárcamo
  - Diego Meléndez Córdova
---

-Instalación de librerías y defaults del proyecto-

```{r setup, echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  cols.print = 5,
  echo    = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  ft.htmlscroll = TRUE
)

library(tidyverse)
library(visdat)
library(dlookr)
library(flextable)
library(skimr)
library(qqplotr)
library(ggpmisc)
library(VIM)
library(mice)
library(gridExtra)

set_flextable_defaults(
  font.size        = 10,
  padding          = 4,
  border.color     = "#cccccc",
  theme_fun        = theme_booktabs,
  digits           = 2,
  decimal.mark     = ",",
  big.mark         = ".",
  na_str           = "—",
)
```

```{r echo = FALSE}
#leer los datos
ai_student_impact<-read.csv("ai_student_impact_dataset.csv",header = TRUE)
```

### Imprimir las primeras filas

```{r}
flextable(head(ai_student_impact))
```

### Variables del set de datos

```{r}
str(ai_student_impact)
```
### Diagnóstico (ver si hay datos faltantes)
```{r}
diagnose(ai_student_impact) %>% flextable()#diagnostico de los datos
```

### Variables categóricas
```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=16, out.width='100%',  dpi=60}
par(mfrow = c(4, 2))  # establecer 4 filas, 2 columnas

barplot(table(ai_student_impact$Major_Category), main = 'Major Category')
barplot(table(ai_student_impact$Year_of_Study), main = 'Year of Study')
barplot(table(ai_student_impact$Primary_Use_Case), main = 'Primary Use Case')
barplot(table(ai_student_impact$Prompt_Engineering_Skill), main = 'Prompt Engineering Skill')
barplot(table(ai_student_impact$Paid_Subscription), main = 'Paid Subscription')
barplot(table(ai_student_impact$Institutional_Policy), main = 'Institutional Policy')
barplot(table(ai_student_impact$Burnout_Risk_Level), main = 'Burnout Risk Level')

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))  # restablecer 1 columna
```

### Medidas estadísticas para variables numéricas

Usamos skim para poder obtener el resumen de todos las columnas en una sola tabla

```{r}
#Variables numéricas: Pre_Semester_GPA, Weekly_GenAI_Hours, Tool_Diversity,Traditional_Study_Hours,Perceived_AI_Dependency,Anxiety_Level_During_Exams,Post_Semester_GPA, Post_Semester_GPA, Skill_Retention_Score         

data = skim(ai_student_impact,Pre_Semester_GPA, Weekly_GenAI_Hours, Tool_Diversity,Traditional_Study_Hours,Perceived_AI_Dependency,Anxiety_Level_During_Exams,Post_Semester_GPA, Post_Semester_GPA, Skill_Retention_Score)

estadisticos <- data |>
  select(Variable = skim_variable,
         'Mínimo'   =numeric.p0,
         'Cuartil 1'= numeric.p25,
         'Mediana'  = numeric.p50,
         'Media'    = numeric.mean,
         'Q3'       = numeric.p75,
         'Max'      = numeric.p100)

flextable(estadisticos)
```
